full_text:
A) Dissociation:

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase, followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H) by María and Sarah.

Dispase dissociation media included 67.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 1.4 ml Dispase + 700 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C rotating for 30 min, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 53.4 ml DMEM/F12+++, 6 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 600 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C, rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

Dispase dissociated cells:

RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++. RBC lysis was repeated with 7 ml for 5 min + 30 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was resuspended in 20 mL of icPDC media (150 mL of icPDC media was prepared). For cell counting a 1:10 dilution was used.

Sequentially dissociated cells:

RBC lysis with 7 ml for 5 min + 25 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was resuspended in 20 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media. For cell counting a 1:10 dilution was used.

Cell counts: measured cell count * 10 (for dilution) is displayed in the table

B) Biobanking:

Dispase dissociated cells: 5x10^6 cells →because of calculation error only 5x0.5^6 cells were frozen

Sequentially dissociated cells: 5x10^6 cells →because of calculation error 5x0.5^6 cells were frozen

Cells were frozen in 1mL of STEM-CellBanker.

C) Experiment

Fresh cells were used for ADC titration experiment, to determine (sub)optimal concentrations of ADCs that produce measurable biological effects without causing excessive cell death. These concentrations will be used in downstream single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) experiments.

→See 15_Log_file_wet_lab on Google drive
